Title: Deacetylation biocatalysis and elicitation by immobilized Penicillium canescens in Astragalus membranaceus hairy root cultures: towards the enhanced and sustainable production of astragaloside IV

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate-derived triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPC treatment up-regulated AACT transcript during 24–60 h, supporting enhanced astragaloside IV accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AACT is one of eleven AG IV pathway genes monitored under IPC elicitation. 2. Its transcript level increased during 24–60 h after IPC treatment. 3. Up-regulation is associated with enhanced AG IV production in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling of astragaloside IV accumulation under IPC treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript up-regulated in IPC-treated hairy roots from 24 to 60 h, peaking at 60 h trend.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-cultivation with immobilized Penicillium canescens (IPC), optimal 60 h, 30 °C, initial pH 7.0
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as pathway-responsive gene, but no direct enzyme assay or transgenic validation was performed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate-derived triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPC treatment induced HMGS expression during 24–60 h, contributing to elevated astragaloside IV biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMGS transcript increased in response to IPC elicitation. 2. The increase paralleled AG IV accumulation. 3. The gene is part of coordinated activation of the AG IV pathway by IPC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradually stimulated under IPC treatment and reached peak around 60 h.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IPC elicitation in hairy root co-culture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional perturbation in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate-derived triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HMGR was strongly induced by IPC, with a 5.46-fold transcript increase at 60 h, and this induction was linked to enhanced AG IV biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMGR expression was significantly induced by IPC treatment in hairy roots. 2. The highest transcript level at 60 h was 5.46-fold above control. 3. The paper highlights HMGR as the most important rate-limiting enzyme in triterpene biosynthesis and implicates its induction in AG IV enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated from 24 to 60 h after IPC treatment, peaking at 60 h.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IPC elicitation in co-cultured hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the most emphasized endogenous biosynthetic gene in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPC treatment gradually stimulated MK transcription up to 60 h, consistent with increased AG IV accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MK is IPC-responsive in AG IV-producing hairy roots. 2. Transcript levels rose during the effective elicitation window. 3. The increase is associated with activation of triterpene precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradually stimulated, peaking at 60 h under IPC treatment.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPC up-regulated PMK transcript during 24–60 h, supporting elevated AG IV production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMK was transcriptionally induced by IPC. 2. Its expression increased along with AG IV accumulation. 3. It forms part of coordinated activation of the mevalonate route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradual induction to a maximum at 60 h under IPC treatment.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / mevalonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MVD was induced during early IPC treatment but was considerably repressed at 96 h, paralleling the decline in AG IV after prolonged co-culture.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MVD expression rose during IPC elicitation before 60 h. 2. At 96 h it was considerably repressed relative to control. 3. Late repression may reflect excessive elicitation and reduced metabolic performance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated during 24–60 h; repressed at 96 h compared with control.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IPC elicitation, prolonged co-culture to 96 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes late repression under excessive elicitation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / triterpene precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FPS expression was induced by IPC before 60 h but became repressed at 96 h, corresponding to reduced AG IV productivity at prolonged treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FPS belongs to the IPC-responsive AG IV pathway genes. 2. Its expression increased during productive elicitation stages. 3. Expression was considerably repressed at 96 h under prolonged stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during 24–60 h; considerably repressed at 96 h.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / triterpene backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPC gradually stimulated SS transcription to 60 h, consistent with increased AG IV biosynthetic flux.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS transcript was progressively induced under IPC elicitation. 2. Peak expression coincided with maximal AG IV accumulation at 60 h. 3. The result supports enhanced triterpene backbone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradually stimulated, reaching peak at 60 h.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / triterpene backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SE was induced by IPC up to 60 h but was considerably repressed at 96 h, matching the optimal AG IV production window.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SE transcription was progressively enhanced during effective IPC elicitation. 2. Peak expression occurred at 60 h. 3. Expression was considerably repressed at 96 h, consistent with over-elicitation damage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradual increase to 60 h; considerable repression at 96 h.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IPC elicitation with prolonged treatment effects

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Astragaloside IV biosynthetic pathway / triterpene cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CAS transcript was gradually stimulated by IPC to 60 h and later repressed at 96 h, indicating involvement in the elicited AG IV biosynthetic response.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAS was among the genes whose expression gradually increased under IPC treatment up to 60 h. 2. This timing coincided with maximal AG IV production. 3. CAS expression was considerably repressed at 96 h under prolonged elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradually stimulated and peaked at 60 h; considerably repressed at 96 h.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Placed in AG IV pathway by the paper; specific downstream oxidation/glycosylation genes were not individually resolved.
